content.content: /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/.harness/docs/STYLE_GUIDE.md

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Introduction
    27→
    28→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    29→
    30→Key decisions:
    31→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    39→
    40→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41→
    42→```
    43→screens/Home/
    44→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46→└── Models/
    47→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48→```
    49→
    50→**Models/index.d.ts**
    51→```ts
    52→export interface HomeParams {
    53→  welcome?: string;
    54→}
    55→export interface HomeState {
    56→  greeting: string;
    57→  isLoading: boolean;
    58→}
    59→export interface HomeHandlers {
    60→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61→}
    62→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63→```
    64→
    65→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66→```ts
    67→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69→import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70→
    71→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72→  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78→  }, []);
    79→
    80→  const greeting = useMemo(
    81→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82→    [params?.welcome, name]
    83→  );
    84→
    85→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87→  }, []);
    88→
    89→  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90→}
    91→```
    92→
    93→**view.tsx (render)**
    94→```tsx
    95→import React from "react";
    96→import { View } from "react-native";
    97→import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98→import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99→import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100→
   101→type Props = HomeParams;
   102→
   103→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105→
   106→  if (isLoading) {
   107→    return (
   108→      <View>
   109→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110→      </View>
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <View>
   116→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118→    </View>
   119→  );
   120→}
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   126→
   127→```
   128→atomic/atoms/Button/
   129→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131→└── Models/
   132→    └── index.d.ts
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Models/index.d.ts**
   136→```ts
   137→export interface ButtonProps {
   138→  text: string;
   139→  disabled?: boolean;
   140→  onPress?: () => void;
   141→}
   142→export interface ButtonState {
   143→  pressed: boolean;
   144→}
   145→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146→  handlePress: () => void;
   147→}
   148→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149→```
   150→
   151→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152→```ts
   153→import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155→
   156→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158→
   159→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160→    setPressed(true);
   161→    props.onPress?.();
   162→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163→  }, [props.onPress]);
   164→
   165→  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166→}
   167→```
   168→
   169→**view.tsx (render)**
   170→```tsx
   171→import React from "react";
   172→import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173→import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174→import { useButton } from "./index";
   175→import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176→
   177→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179→
   180→  return (
   181→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182→      <View>
   183→        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " •" : ""}</Text>
   184→      </View>
   185→    </Pressable>
   186→  );
   187→}
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Project Organization
   193→
   194→```
   195→src/
   196→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   197→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199→│   ├── atoms/
   200→│   ├── molecules/
   201→│   └── organisms/
   202→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   207→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   210→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   215→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   217→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219→```
   220→
   221→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Services Architecture
   226→
   227→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   228→
   229→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   230→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   231→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   233→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   234→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   235→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   236→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   237→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238→
   239→Principles:
   240→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241→- No circular dependencies.
   242→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247→
   248→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   249→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   250→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   251→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252→
   253→**Slice example**
   254→```ts
   255→import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257→
   258→export type AuthSlice = {
   259→    auth: AuthState;
   260→    authActions: AuthActions
   261→};
   262→
   263→const initialAuth = {
   264→    user: null,
   265→    loading: false,
   266→    error: undefined,
   267→}
   268→
   269→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270→    auth: initialAuth,
   271→    authActions: {
   272→        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273→        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274→        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275→        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276→    }
   277→});
   278→```
   279→
   280→**Root Store**
   281→
   282→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   283→
   284→```ts
   285→import { create } from "zustand";
   286→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287→import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288→
   289→import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291→
   292→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293→
   294→/** Add your slice Type */
   295→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296→
   297→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298→  devtools(
   299→    persist(
   300→      immer((...a) => ({
   301→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303→      })),
   304→      {
   305→        name: "root-storage",
   306→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307→        partialize: (s) => ({
   308→          user: s.user,
   309→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310→        }),
   311→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312→      }
   313→    ),
   314→    { name: "RootStore" }
   315→  )
   316→);
   317→```
   318→
   319→- For Use on Class.
   320→
   321→```ts
   322→class Auth {
   323→  private readonly setAuth;
   324→
   325→  constructor () {
   326→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  function doSomething(data) {
   330→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331→  }
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   337→
   338→```ts
   339→const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340→const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341→```
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Routes Naming
   346→
   347→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   348→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   349→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Folder Naming Rules
   354→
   355→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356→|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359→
   360→---
   361→
   362→## Code Standards
   363→
   364→**Language**: English only.  
   365→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366→
   367→**Explanatory variables**
   368→```js
   369→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370→const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371→const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372→isNumber(1);
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Semantic constants**
   376→```js
   377→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379→```
   380→
   381→**Library workarounds with link**
   382→```js
   383→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385→event.target.value = "";
   386→```
   387→
   388→**JSDoc**
   389→```js
   390→/**
   391→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392→ * @param {*} eventName
   393→ * @param {*} maxLength
   394→ */
   395→```
   396→
   397→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   398→```ts
   399→useEffect(() => {
   400→  (async () => {
   401→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402→    setSum(42);
   403→  })();
   404→}, [
   405→  setSum,
   406→]);
   407→```
   408→
   409→**Extract conditions**
   410→```ts
   411→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Optional chaining**
   416→```ts
   417→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418→```
   419→
   420→**JSX indentation**
   421→```tsx
   422→{
   423→  !isEnd && (
   424→    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425→  )
   426→}
   427→```
   428→
   429→**Early returns**
   430→```ts
   431→if (!data) return null;
   432→return <View />;
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Nullish coalescing**
   436→```ts
   437→return data ?? 0;
   438→```
   439→---
   440→
   441→## Styles Patterns
   442→
   443→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   444→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→## Cache Pattern
   449→
   450→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451→
   452→```ts
   453→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454→
   455→export interface CachedData<T> {
   456→  timestamp: number;
   457→  data: T;
   458→}
   459→
   460→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464→}
   465→
   466→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468→
   469→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471→    if (!json) return null;
   472→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476→    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482→  }
   483→}
   484→```
   485→
   486→**Guidelines**
   487→- One storage class per module/feature.
   488→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492→
   493→---
   494→
   495→## Checklist
   496→
   497→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504→
   505→---
   506→
   507→Happy shipping! 🚀

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me provide a comprehensive analysis.

## Analysis Summary: ExtractRewardProgram Screen Architecture

### 1. Screen Structure — Hook Component Pattern Compliance

**Status: COMPLIANT** ✓

The screen follows the Hook Component pattern precisely:

- **`index.ts`** (Logic Hook): Exports `useExtractRewardProgramScreen()` hook containing all business logic, state management, and event handlers
- **`view.tsx`** (Render): Pure presentational component that destructures hook return value and renders UI
- **`Models/index.ts`** (Types): Centralized type definitions with clear separation:
  - `ExtractRewardProgramState` — UI state
  - `ExtractRewardProgramHandlers` — All handler callbacks
  - `UseExtractRewardProgramReturn` — Combined state + handlers
  - `ExtractRewardProgramProps` — Screen navigation props
  - Helper interfaces (`FilterGroup`, `EmptyListProps`, `TransactionsListProps`)

**Structure matches STYLE_GUIDE exactly:**
```
ExtractRewardProgram/
├── Models/index.ts        # Types
├── index.ts               # useExtractRewardProgramScreen() hook
├── view.tsx               # FC<ExtractRewardProgramProps>
├── utils.ts               # Helper functions
└── data/index.ts          # Constants (SCREEN_STRINGS, ANALYTICS_EVENTS)
```

---

### 2. Dependencies and Imports

**Hook dependencies (index.ts line 1-26):**

| Layer | Import | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| **React Core** | `useCallback`, `useEffect`, `useMemo`, `useRef`, `useState` | Standard React hooks |
| **React Native** | `InteractionManager`, `NativeSyntheticEvent`, `NativeScrollEvent` | Performance & scroll tracking |
| **Navigation** | `useFocusEffect` | Screen focus lifecycle |
| **Zustand** | `useShallow` | Shallow store selector |
| **Services** | `getPointsExtract`, `getPointsBalance` | Domain service layer |
| **Store** | `useRootStore` | Zustand root state |
| **Repository** | `processPointsExtractData` | Data transformation |
| **Hooks** | `useDebounce` | Custom hook for search debouncing |
| **Models** | `PointsExtractStatus`, `PointsBalanceStatus`, `AppliedFilters`, etc. | Domain types |

**Critical: Proper `~/` usage**
- Line 23: `useRootStore` correctly accessed from `~/store` (SuperApp integration) ✓

**View.tsx dependencies:**
- Design System: `@semparar/design-system-app` (CardPoints, SearchUsed, Tabs, etc.)
- Atomic components: PointsInfoBottomSheet, FiltersBottomSheet, BasicScreen, ApiError
- All properly decoupled from business logic

### 3. Business Logic and Handlers

**State Management (lines 30-108):**

The hook manages:

1. **Refs for Performance (lines 33-39):**
   - `hasTrackedScreenView`, `hasTrackedSearchPress` — Prevent duplicate analytics
   - `canLoadMoreRef`, `isLoadingMoreRef` — Ref-based scroll loading state

2. **Local State (lines 41-43):**
   - `isRefreshing` — Pull-to-refresh indicator
   - `transactionsData` — Rendered transaction list
   - `searchText` + `debouncedSearchText` — Debounced search (400ms)

3. **Store Selectors (lines 45-58):**
   - `useShallow` for shallow equality to prevent unnecessary re-renders
   - Extracts: `pointsExtract`, `pointsBalance`, `pointsExtractFilters`, `pointsExtractActions`

**Handlers Breakdown:**

| Handler | Purpose | Implementation |
|---------|---------|-----------------|
| `handleGoBack` | Navigation | Calls `params?.navigation?.goBack()` |
| `requestPointsExtract` | Fetch data | Calls `getPointsExtract({ page: 1 })` |
| `handleLoadMore` | Pagination | Increments page if `currentPage < totalPages` |
| `setTransactionsFilters` | Apply filters | Updates store via `pointsExtractActions` |
| `clearTransactionsFilters` | Reset filters | Clears store state |
| `handleSearchChange` | Debounced search | Updates local `searchText` |
| `handleRefresh` | Pull-to-refresh | Sets `isRefreshing` + calls `requestPointsExtract()` |
| `handleError` | Retry logic | Calls `requestPointsExtract()` again |
| `handleScroll` | Infinite scroll | Checks distance from bottom (300px threshold), triggers load more |
| `handleIconInfoPress` | Modal trigger | Opens info bottom sheet + tracks analytics |
| `handleFilterIconPress` | Filter modal | Opens filters bottom sheet + tracks analytics |
| `handleApplyFilters` | Filter submission | Maps UI indices to period/types + updates store |
| `handleTabChange` | Tab switching | Tracks analytics for "Todos" vs "Expirados" tabs |
| `handleSearchPressIn` | Search interaction | One-time analytics tracking |

**Effects (lines 60-215):**

1. **Screen view tracking (lines 60-80):** Once-per-mount analytics
2. **Initial data fetch (lines 82-92):** Fetches balance and extract on mount/dependency change
3. **Refresh state cleanup (lines 94-98):** Resets `isRefreshing` when loading completes
4. **Search text reset (lines 100-104):** Clears search press tracking
5. **Transaction filtering (lines 142-215):** Complex memoized chain:
   - `getTransactionsWithFilters` → applies period + type filters
   - `getExtractData` → merges filtered transactions
   - `getTransactionsData` → applies text-based search (debounced)
   - `InteractionManager.runAfterInteractions()` → processes to mapped format
   - Cleanup: Cancels task if component unmounts

**Memoization Strategy:**

```
pointsExtract.extractData 
  → getTransactionsWithFilters (with appliedTransactionsFilters)
    → getExtractData (memoized)
      → getTransactionsData (search applied)
        → processPointsExtractData (via InteractionManager)
          → setTransactionsData (rendered)
```

---

### 4. SOLID Architecture Implementation

**Single Responsibility Principle:**

- **API Layer** (`extract.api.ts`): Raw HTTP calls, no logic
  - `getPointsExtractApi()` — POST to backend
  - `getPointsBalanceApi()` — GET balance
  - Request payload building isolated in `buildExtractRequestPayload()`

- **Models** (`extract.model.ts`): Types only
  - Enums: `PointsExtractStatus`, `PointsBalanceStatus`
  - DTOs: `PointsExtractApiResponse`, `TransactionDto`
  - Domain: `PointsTransaction`, `PointsExtractData`, `AppliedFilters`

- **Repository** (`extract.repository.ts`): Data transformation
  - `getPointsExtractFromResponse()` — Maps API → domain model
  - `processPointsExtractData()` — Groups by date, formats for UI
  - Type mapping: `TRANSACTION_TYPE_MAP` (Credito → CREDIT, etc.)
  - Formatting: Date localization, points value display (+/- prefix)
  - **Class-based mapper**: `PointsDataGroupedByDayMapper` — Groups and labels transactions

- **Service** (`extract.service.ts`): Orchestration
  - `getPointsExtract()` — Coordinates API → repository → store
  - `getPointsBalance()` — Manages balance fetch lifecycle
  - Guards: `shouldFetchExtract()`, `shouldFetchBalance()` prevent duplicate requests
  - Error handlers: Separate success/error paths for first page vs pagination
  - Analytics integration: `eventServerResponse()` for tracking

- **Store** (`extract.store.ts`): State mutations only
  - Actions are pure functions (no side effects)
  - `setExtractData()` — Replace entire extract (first page)
  - `appendExtractData()` — Merge with existing (pagination)
  - `setTransactionsFilters()` — Update filter state
  - State reset available

**Dependency Inversion:**

- View depends on hook, not directly on store or services
- Services use store via `useRootStore.getState()` (correct for non-React context)
- Hook orchestrates: coordinates services + store + local state

**Open/Closed:**

- Easy to add new filter types (just extend `AppliedFilters`)
- Analytics events centralized in `data/index.ts` (maintainable)
- Filter groups defined as data (not hardcoded in render)

---

### 5. Issues and Pattern Misalignments

**CRITICAL ISSUES:**

1. **Missing Error State Display (Lines 95-103, view.tsx)**
   - `hasError` is hardcoded to `false` in hook (line 371)
   - Error UI exists but never shown
   - **Should implement**: Error handling when `pointsExtract.status === ERROR`

   ```typescript
   // Current (line 371):
   hasError: false,
   
   // Should be:
   hasError: pointsExtract.status === PointsExtractStatus.ERROR
   ```

2. **Repository Data Mapping Issue (extract.repository.ts, lines 187-188)**
   - `getTransactionFromDto()` doesn't assign `marketplace` field
   - Creates incomplete `PointsTransaction` objects missing marketplace data
   - **Impact**: Marketplace order details (vendors, items) discarded from API response

   ```typescript
   // Line 187-188 missing:
   marketplace: dto.marketplace || undefined,
   ```

3. **Search Implementation Pattern Mismatch (view.tsx, line 174)**
   - Tab component includes search in "Todos" tab only
   - **Second tab "A expirar"** is empty JSX (`<></>`)
   - **Issue**: Search logic in hook applies to both, but UI only shows for first tab
   - **Design question**: Should expiring tab have separate search/filter logic?

4. **Type Inconsistency in Repository (extract.repository.ts, line 188)**
   - `status: dto.status` assigned directly without type validation
   - Should validate against expected status values

5. **Debounce Implementation (index.ts, line 43)**
   - `useDebounce` hook imported but not exported from utils
   - Tight coupling to specific debounce implementation
   - **Better**: Could parametrize delay or use custom hook pattern

**MINOR PATTERN DEVIATIONS:**

6. **Inline Constants in Filter Groups (index.ts, lines 332-351)**
   - Filter labels hardcoded in useMemo
   - **STYLE_GUIDE expectation**: Constants should be in `data/index.ts` or `constants.ts`
   - Currently works but reduces maintainability

   ```typescript
   // Current:
   const getFilterGroups: FilterGroup[] = useMemo(() => {
     return [{
       label: 'Períodos',
       options: [
         { label: '7 dias', value: 0 },
         ...
   
   // Better:
   // Move to data/index.ts
   export const FILTER_GROUPS = [...]
   ```

7. **Mixed Concerns in Data Processing (index.ts, lines 161-215)**
   - `getTransactionsWithFilters()` does filtering but doesn't account for pagination state
   - Filtered results are re-processed every render when deps change
   - **Performance**: Could be optimized with request-time filtering on backend

8. **Analytics Tracking Conditions (index.ts, lines 325-330)**
   - Search press tracking uses mutable ref `hasTrackedSearchPress`
   - Logic resets in useEffect (line 102) but only if searchText becomes empty
   - **Edge case**: If user types → deletes → types again, second press not tracked

9. **Filter UI Index Mapping (index.ts, lines 286-303)**
   - Hardcoded filter indices: `filters[0]` = period, `filters[1]` = type
   - Brittle; renaming/reordering breaks logic
   - **Better**: Named keys or type-safe mapping

**STYLE_GUIDE COMPLIANCE CHECK:**

| Checklist Item | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Hook Component pattern | ✓ | Properly split |
| Types in Models/ | ✓ | Complete type hierarchy |
| Zustand only | ✓ | Correct usage with `~/store` |
| UI never calls API | ✓ | Services layer enforced |
| Code standards | ⚠ | Some hardcoded values should be constants |
| Cache pattern | N/A | Not applicable for extract |
| Routes naming | ✓ | Uses PascalCase |
| Folder naming | ✓ | camelCase for services, PascalCase for screens |
